title: resolvePromptField heuristic misclassifies inline prompts as template file paths
description: When an inline prompt string (prompt.system or prompt.task) is a single line containing any '/' character, resolvePromptField classifies it as a template file path instead of inline text. The condition `value.includes('/') && !value.includes('\n')` triggers on natural-language prompts that mention directories (e.g. 'docs/03-solar-engine.md'), URLs, or any path-like string. There is no explicit 'inline:' discriminator in the YAML shape — only 'template:' exists as an explicit key. The inline path is heuristic-only, and the heuristic is wrong.
actual_behavior: Prompt text like 'Read docs/03-solar-engine.md' is stored as taskPromptTemplate instead of taskPrompt. compileAgent calls renderTemplateFile with the prompt text as a file path, resulting in ENOENT. The compile fails with 'no prompt content produced' when both prompts are misclassified.
expected_behavior: Inline prompts containing natural language that happens to mention paths should be treated as inline text, not template file paths. A conservative heuristic would only treat a value as a template path if it ENDS with a known template extension (.md, .txt) and does not contain natural-language markers (spaces, punctuation outside path characters). Alternatively, an explicit 'inline:' key should exist alongside 'template:' in the YAML shape.
reproduction: 1. author-agent-spec with prompt.task set to any string containing '/' and no newlines (e.g. 'Read the docs at /Users/me/project/docs/'). 2. call-agent on that spec. 3. Observe ENOENT trying to open the prompt text as a file path.
workaround: Remove all '/' characters from inline prompts. Use template variables like {{ input.doc }} to pass paths instead of hardcoding them.
